name: ITIL v4 Incident Management
description: IT Infrastructure Library framework for IT service management

controls.title: Incident Logging and Classification
controls.description: All incidents must be logged and classified using agreed categorization scheme

requirements.description: Incidents must be logged with unique ID, timestamp, reporter, and initial category

evidence_requirements: Incident record with all mandatory fields
controls.failure_consequence: Non-compliance with ITIL incident management practice

controls.title: Incident Prioritization
controls.description: Priority must be determined using impact x urgency matrix

requirements.description: Priority determined using impact x urgency matrix

controls.failure_consequence: SLA breach risk - incorrect priority leads to wrong response times

controls.title: Change Management for Incident Resolution
controls.description: All changes to resolve incidents must follow change management process

requirements.description: All changes recorded as change records
Emergency changes follow expedited change approval process

controls.failure_consequence: Uncontrolled changes - risk of further service degradation

controls.title: Known Error Database Check
controls.description: Known error database must be consulted before investigation

requirements.description: Known error database consulted before investigation

controls.failure_consequence: Wasted investigation time on already-known issues

controls.title: Post-Incident Review
controls.description: Major incidents must have formal post-incident review

requirements.description: Formal post-incident review within 5 business days
Review documents root cause, timeline, and improvement actions

evidence_requirements: Action items with owners and deadlines
controls.failure_consequence: Failure to learn from incidents - recurring service disruptions
